method_notes.va_awards: USAspending API POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_award/ with filters psc_codes=['V225'] (Ambulance Service), award_type_codes=['A','B','C','D'], time_period 2022-10-01 through 2025-09-30 (FY2023-FY2025 activity window), sorted by Award Amount descending, 3 pages x 100. Top 15 by award amount flagged top15_document_lookup_flag for document lookup by PIID. 261 of 300 awards are Department of Veterans Affairs; remainder DoD, State, HHS, Peace Corps under the same PSC.
method_notes.public_contracts: State portals probed: Nebraska statecontracts.nebraska.gov (server-rendered, searchable, documents downloadable), Iowa bidopportunities.iowa.gov (JS), Kansas admin.ks.gov contract search (PeopleSoft login), MissouriBUYS (JS). Additional directly fetchable public contract PDFs located via web search of municipal and county repositories. Fields are null when the fetched document does not state them.

per_trip_rates_by_level.non_ambulance_client_transport: USD 39.02 per one-way trip or USD 2.00 per mile, whichever is greater, wholly within Douglas and Sarpy counties; USD 25.00 per one-way trip or USD 2.00 per mile, whichever is greater, wholly within the originating city or village limits in all other counties; USD 2.00 per mile beyond originating corporate limits; Transportation Escort service USD 17.06 per hour

public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: Renewal 1 total amount USD 3,320,000.00; database lists contract total USD 6,640,000.00 (original plus executed renewal periods)
public_contracts.escalator_mechanics_and_dates: No indexed escalator; rates revised by attachment (rate attachments dated 'Revised July 1, 2025'); statutory cap of no greater than three times the state employee mileage rate for out-of-corporate-limits transport per Neb. Rev. Stat. 75-303.03
public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Original term July 1, 2023 through June 30, 2025; Renewal 1 July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2027; renewal number one of one, none remaining
public_contracts.penalty_structure: No-show payments to contractor scaled by scheduled trip distance (Service Code 1014); no liquidated damages on contractor stated in fetched pages
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: Common carrier licensed with Nebraska Public Service Commission; vehicle and staffing standards per contract attachments
public_contracts.issuer: State of Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
public_contracts.url: https://statecontracts.nebraska.gov/ (contract 104886-O4, vendor Camelot Transportation Inc; document download links on the database are session-tokenized)
public_contracts.doc_type: executed state services contract renewal with rate attachments
public_contracts.notes: Child-welfare and client transportation (non-emergency, non-ambulance) for DHHS; recorded because it is a state medical-adjacent patient transport contract with published unit rates. Local copy: pdfs/ne_camelot_104886_0.pdf

per_trip_rates_by_level.non_ambulance_client_transport: USD 2.10 per mile beyond originating corporate limits and USD 2.10 per mile wholly within originating corporate limits per rate attachment; no-show payments scaled by scheduled trip distance

public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Contract begins October 1, 2025 and ends June 30, 2027; optional renewals: zero
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: Vehicles equipped with operable seat belts, turn signals, lights, and horn per Section 1.7; Nebraska PSC licensure
public_contracts.technology_platform_fees: Claims submitted as N-FOCUS generated electronic claims through the DHHS web portal; no platform fee stated
public_contracts.issuer: State of Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
public_contracts.url: https://statecontracts.nebraska.gov/ (contract 114283-O4, vendor Snowleopard Medical Transport; document download links are session-tokenized)
public_contracts.doc_type: executed state services contract with rate attachments
public_contracts.notes: Non-emergency medical/client transportation. Local copy: pdfs/ne_snowleopard_114283_0.pdf

public_contracts.dedicated_unit_or_unit_hour_rate: One 24-hour ALS ambulance and one 24-hour on-call BLS ambulance provided by AMR (TEK Inc. dba American Medical Response)
public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: Operational subsidy (Agency Contribution): Year 1 USD 415,000; Year 2 USD 427,450; Year 3 USD 440,273; split 50/50 between city and county; Floyd County Medical Center pledged USD 100,000 toward year-one operation, split evenly between city and county, bringing year-one city cost to USD 157,500
public_contracts.escalator_mechanics_and_dates: 3 percent annual increases: USD 415,000 (FY beginning 7/1/2023) to USD 427,450 to USD 440,273
public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Initial term 3 years, 7/1/2023 through 6/30/2026; renewal by written consent of Agency and AMR
public_contracts.exclusivity_or_first_call: Agreement covers emergency medical services to Charles City, St. Charles Township and Floyd County for the term; termination for convenience on 180-day notice (changed from prior agreement)
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: AMR will upgrade and replace ambulances and defibrillators; ambulance personnel are employees of AMR
public_contracts.issuer: City of Charles City and Floyd County, Iowa (council agenda item approving renewal)
public_contracts.date: Approved at June 14, 2023 council meeting; term from 2023-07-01
public_contracts.doc_type: executed ambulance services agreement (renewal) with council cover memo
public_contracts.notes: AMR bills patients and establishes payment plans case by case; monthly reporting includes hospital-to-hospital transfer trip numbers; annual capital equipment plan due 60 days before each anniversary. Local copy: pdfs/charlescity_amr_2023.pdf

public_contracts.dedicated_unit_or_unit_hour_rate: Two ALS ambulances dedicated to the System around the clock during the term; supervisor vehicle (S3/S4) staffed with one ALS-level paramedic supervisor
public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: Annual subsidy USD 650,000.00 paid quarterly; each of the five towns pays USD 32,500.00 per quarter (USD 130,000.00 per town per year)
public_contracts.escalator_mechanics_and_dates: If the one additional two-year term is exercised, built-in 2.5 percent escalator: USD 666,250.00 then USD 682,906.25 for the successive years as printed in the agreement
public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Twelve months from March 1, 2024; option to renew for one additional two-year term by mutual agreement; Pridestar may terminate remaining town agreements if towns within the System terminate
public_contracts.exclusivity_or_first_call: Pridestar responds within the System; ALS intercept protocol: when a town paramedic intercepts a Pridestar BLS unit, Pridestar bills for ALS services and reimburses the intercepting town the difference between an ALS and BLS call per transport
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: One-for-one replacement of equipment between Pridestar EMS and on-scene personnel
public_contracts.issuer: Towns of Atkinson, Danville, Hampstead, Newton and Sandown, New Hampshire (the System)
public_contracts.doc_type: executed multi-town ambulance services agreement (Pridestar EMS Inc. of Massachusetts)
public_contracts.notes: Pridestar bills patients for emergency medical services. File is posted on the Town of Plaistow document center under a legacy Trinity label; the executed agreement text names Pridestar EMS Inc. Local copy: pdfs/plaistow_trinity_2024.pdf

public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: AMR pays the city USD 200.00 per transport for each Topeka Fire Department response to an AMR request for a non-emergency lift assist attributable to patient weight
public_contracts.escalator_mechanics_and_dates: Rate relief mechanism: if AMR does not earn a fair rate of return (revenues from patient charges and public funds) over six months, remedies may include rate increases and/or service reductions per ordinance process
public_contracts.response_time_SLA: AMR shall respond to city requests for reports including ALS/BLS response, response times, unit and triage data; response time performance standards per current Shawnee County contract framework
public_contracts.penalty_structure: USD 100.00 per week penalty until requested report information is provided; USD 100.00 per day penalty for annual January submission until submitted; material fraud or default is grounds for forfeiture of the franchise
public_contracts.issuer: City of Topeka, Kansas (Ordinance No. 20331, published December 20, 2021)
public_contracts.doc_type: city franchise ordinance for ambulance service (American Medical Response)

public_contracts.response_time_SLA: Monthly response time compliance is measured and published per Resolution 2023-14; report verifies AMR compliance with all requirements and parameters of Contract C207-2016 as amended for FY2023
public_contracts.issuer: Shawnee County, Kansas Ambulance Compliance Officer
public_contracts.doc_type: county compliance report on ambulance contractor performance (AMR under Contract C207-2016, as amended; Resolution 2023-14)
public_contracts.notes: Underlying Contract C207-2016 document was not located online; county resolutions 2024-35 and 2025-33 are posted as scanned PDFs (no embedded text). Local copy: pdfs/snco_amr_fy23.pdf

per_trip_rates_by_level.note: All transports billed at levels approved in City Ordinance; independent audit reviews pricing compliance and CMS billing compliance

public_contracts.response_time_SLA: 911: Priority 1 paramedic ambulance on scene within 8:59 for at least 90 percent of responses; Priority 2 within 11:59 at 90 percent; Priority 3 within 15:59 at 90 percent. Interfacility: Priority 4 scheduled interfacility requests require paramedic ambulance within 30 minutes of requested pickup 90 percent of the time; Priority 5 unscheduled interfacility requests have a 90-minute paramedic response goal (customer service target, not a compliance requirement)
public_contracts.penalty_structure: Monthly response-time compliance reporting against the 90 percent standards; contractor was compliant with all response time requirements each month of the contract year
public_contracts.technology_platform_fees: Response time reports and call audits administered through the contract oversight program (Sioux Falls Regional Emergency Medical Services); no vendor platform fee stated in report
public_contracts.issuer: City of Sioux Falls, South Dakota (Health Department report to City Council)
public_contracts.date: Contract year ending May 20, 2024; submitted July 16, 2024
public_contracts.doc_type: annual ambulance contract performance report (contractor Paramedics Plus, now PatientCare EMS)
public_contracts.notes: Report covers pricing compliance, response time performance, reporting compliance. Direct curl fetch returns HTTP 403; retrieved via browser-equivalent fetch. Local copy: pdfs/siouxfalls_perf_2024.pdf

public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: Template provides an expense allocation formula section, including per-capita allocation based on population within each municipality's service area
public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Template: initial term of ____ years with optional automatic renewal for additional term(s)
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: Cost sharing for ambulances amortized over vehicle life span and for high-cost equipment of at least USD 5,000
public_contracts.issuer: University of Wisconsin-Madison Extension Local Government Center
public_contracts.doc_type: model annotated EMS agreement (template for Wisconsin municipal ambulance service contracting)
public_contracts.notes: Not an executed contract; included as the reference structure Wisconsin municipalities use for shared ambulance service agreements. Local copy: pdfs/wi_model_ems_2024.pdf

per_trip_rates_by_level.note: Patient charges per Fee Schedule at Exhibit B of the agreement; exhibit rate table not included in the fetched packet text layer

public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Sixty months, February 1, 2018 through January 31, 2023; city may extend up to 120 days beyond the term
public_contracts.exclusivity_or_first_call: Operator dispatched through City PSAP using Priority Dispatch System; direct-line emergency calls routed through city dispatch
public_contracts.response_time_SLA: Fractile measurement to the 90th percentile; citywide 8-minute Priority 1 response standard in urban zones; twelve response zones per City Response Zones Map; official clock is the City Communication Center; first-arriving qualified resource stops the clock
public_contracts.penalty_structure: Performance security of USD 1,000,000; liquidated damages per Exhibit D schedule (stated as reasonable estimates of damages, not penalties), payable 30 days from invoice; termination for cause awards liquidated damages in the amount of the performance security
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: Operator provides and replaces its equipment, materials and supplies (Exhibit E minimum equipment schedule); operator reimburses city for supplies consumed; operator resupplies Pueblo Fire Department expendable medical supplies; city cost requests capped at USD 10,000 per year; USD 3,000 per ambulance per year cap on certain required expenditures
public_contracts.doc_type: executed ambulance service agreement (American Medical Response of Colorado) via competitive bid
public_contracts.notes: Insurance: USD 1M/3M general, USD 3M/5M professional, USD 5M excess. Local copy: pdfs/pueblo_amb_agreement.pdf

per_trip_rates_by_level.note: Pafford sets ambulance rates annually and makes them publicly available; approved base rate and itemized charges must be consistent with rates authorized by Medicaid, Medicare and other applicable payers

public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: RFP framework requires any contractor needing a subsidy to disclose the amount and derivation; no subsidy amount stated in the ordinance packet
public_contracts.term_and_renewal: Contract commenced February 1, 2022 for Saline County, Alexander and other named parties; after the initial period the contract automatically renews for successive three-year periods, subject to performance on response-time reliability, economic efficiency, and customer satisfaction
public_contracts.exclusivity_or_first_call: Exclusive franchise to operate and maintain ambulance service in the city; exclusive contract for emergency medical services during the term, covering transports from facilities including hospitals, nursing homes, and physician offices
public_contracts.response_time_SLA: Exhibit B: Zone 1 emergency 8 minutes 59 seconds, non-emergent 11 minutes 59 seconds; Zone 2 emergency 11 minutes 59 seconds, non-emergent 14 minutes 59 seconds; hospital transfer (emergent) 30 minutes; hospital transfer (non-emergent) 60 minutes
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: Operations integrated with AWIN radio system in Saline County; insurance minimums USD 2,000,000 per occurrence (multiple coverages), USD 5,000,000 aggregate professional
public_contracts.issuer: City of Benton, Arkansas (Ordinance No. 41 of 2025, extending franchise; underlying Amended Agreement with Saline County parties)
public_contracts.doc_type: city ordinance extending exclusive ambulance franchise (Pafford Medical Services Inc) with agreement attached

public_contracts.term_and_renewal: July 1, 2025 through December 31, 2029; Board may renew for up to two additional five-year terms on written notice; 90-day transition period before termination date
public_contracts.exclusivity_or_first_call: Franchise grants exclusive provision of emergency ambulance services within Ambulance Service Area #5 per the county ASA Plan
public_contracts.response_time_SLA: Franchisee must comply with maximum allowable system response times on the ASA Map and achieve 90 percent response time compliance in the franchise area each month; response time measured from dispatch, ambulance arrival at scene stops the clock; frontier zone measurement variant
public_contracts.penalty_structure: Liquidated damages: USD 50.00 for each one-tenth of a percentage point below the monthly response-time compliance standard; USD 500.00 for missing required equipment or supplies on any ambulance; USD 500.00 for reporting arrival on scene before actual arrival; USD 250.00 for material failure to notify the Board within 10 business days of equipment or staffing falling below standards; USD 250.00 for failure to notify the ASA administrator within 10 business days of specified events including mechanical failure with a patient on board
public_contracts.equipment_responsibility: BLS ambulance consistent with state standard; ALS staffing minimums specified; dedicated event ambulances may be provided and charged separately; insurance minimum USD 2,000,000 per occurrence and aggregate
public_contracts.issuer: Columbia County, Oregon Board of County Commissioners (Ambulance Service Area #5)
public_contracts.date: Term commencing 2025-07-01 (agreement C75-2025, June 2025 board agenda)
public_contracts.doc_type: executed ambulance service franchise agreement (franchisee Clatskanie Rural Fire Protection District)

per_trip_rates_by_level.ALS: Ambulance base rate USD 2,431, a 5.5 percent reduction from the prior rate of USD 2,574

public_contracts.minimum_commitment_or_subsidy: No county subsidy; contractor pays county and JPA fees listed under technology_platform_fees
public_contracts.term_and_renewal: July 1, 2019 through June 30, 2024, with extension opportunity; county historically re-procures approximately every 10 years
public_contracts.exclusivity_or_first_call: Exclusive Operating Area (EOA) for emergency ambulance service with ALS transport under California Health and Safety Code 1797.85, awarded through July 2018 RFP
public_contracts.response_time_SLA: Must meet or exceed the response time standard for each zone on 90 percent of 911 emergency medical calls; recent performance 93 percent against the 90 percent contractual obligation
public_contracts.technology_platform_fees: Contractor-funded system fees per year: dispatch fees USD 1,038,734; CAD maintenance USD 60,000 to 100,000; radio system maintenance USD 131,144; EMS agency oversight and monitoring USD 941,130; pager fees USD 24,000; JPA first responder fees USD 4,815,508; AMR also funds an Online Compliance Utility program for response time oversight and FirstPass, and an operational data analyst position
public_contracts.issuer: County of San Mateo, California (Board resolution and agreement, May 14, 2019)
public_contracts.doc_type: executed exclusive operating area agreement for 911 ALS ambulance transport (American Medical Response)
public_contracts.notes: Large-market EOA benchmark included for rate and fee structure comparison. Local copy: pdfs/smc_amr_2019.pdf

parked.source: SAM.gov (contract opportunity documents for VA V225 solicitations, e.g. price schedules for PIIDs flagged top15_document_lookup_flag)
parked.blocker: SAM.gov API requires an API key for opportunity and attachment retrieval; award documents not fetchable anonymously. USAspending award metadata collected instead.

parked.source: San Joaquin County CA EMS Agency, AMR ambulance agreement 2021
parked.blocker: PDF downloaded (74 pages) but is a scanner image file with no text layer; no OCR tool available in this environment. Local copy pdfs/sj_amr_2021.pdf

parked.blocker: Scanned PDF with no text layer; no OCR available. Local copy pdfs/walkerga_amb.pdf

parked.source: Shawnee County KS ambulance contract C207-2016 (as amended) and Resolutions 2024-35 / 2025-33
parked.blocker: Contract document itself is not posted on the county compliance page; posted resolutions are scanned PDFs without a text layer (no OCR available). FY23 compliance report abstracted instead

parked.source: Nebraska state contracts database, DHHS payment rows to county and volunteer ambulance squads (e.g. Hamilton County Ambulance, Mullen Ambulance Service, Dundy County Ambulance)
parked.blocker: Rows are small dollar payment records, not contract documents with rate schedules; retained in scratch ne_results_raw.json, not abstracted

parked.source: Hospital-to-hospital / interfacility transport agreements between private health systems and private ambulance companies
parked.blocker: Agreements between two private parties are not public records; only public-entity contracts (municipal, county, state, federal) and public board packets are obtainable. Public proxies collected instead: Sioux Falls Priority 4/5 interfacility SLAs, Benton AR hospital transfer response times, Charles City hospital-to-hospital transfer reporting clause
